short_summary: Regional tensions, no direct EU threat
rationale: The most relevant events for a Polish user are the ongoing US-Iran conflict (indirect energy/price risk), Ukraine's deep strike into Russia (war escalation potential near Poland), and Israeli strikes in Lebanon — all are continuations of known tensions without a qualitative leap directly threatening Poland or NATO.

top_events.title: Ukraine strikes military plant 900 km inside Russia
top_events.summary: Ukraine used its domestically produced 'Flamingo' missile to hit a target deep inside Russian territory. This represents a qualitative escalation in Ukraine's long-range strike capability, which could provoke Russian retaliation and increases the risk of broader conflict near Poland's eastern border.

top_events.title: US-Iran conflict: strikes exchanged, new threats
top_events.summary: The US and Iran have exchanged strikes and are trading new threats, including reported US strikes on Iranian water infrastructure. Prolonged US-Iran conflict poses indirect risk to Poland via potential energy price spikes and broader Middle East destabilization.

top_events.title: Israeli strikes kill 17 in southern Lebanon
top_events.summary: Israel conducted strikes in southern Lebanon killing at least 17 people. This continues the pattern of Israeli military action in the region; no direct impact on Poland, but contributes to broader Middle East instability with indirect effects on energy markets.
